revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 加茂神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=さいたま市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]4-8-1 |地図 = |祭神 = [[賀茂別雷命|別雷神]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]]…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 加茂神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=さいたま市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]4-8-1 |地図 = |祭神 = [[賀茂別雷命|別雷神]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]」の「宮」の由来であり、[[山城国]][[一宮]]の[[賀茂別雷神社]]の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の[[吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|吉祥院]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。
[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の神社が合祀された。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[宮原駅]]より徒歩8分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしや}} [[Category:さいたま市の神社]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の歴史]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社]]

== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は[[宮原村 (埼玉県)|宮原村]]および[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]を参照。</ref>、[[山城国]][[一宮]]の[[賀茂別雷神社]]の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の[[吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|吉祥院]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。
[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の神社が合祀された。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == * [[宮原駅]]より徒歩8分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
== 関連項目 == * [[上尾宿]] * [[中山道]]

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{神社 |名称 = 加茂神社 |画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]4-8-1 |位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = |祭神 = [[賀茂別雷命|別雷神]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は[[宮原村 (埼玉県)|宮原村]]および[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]を参照。</ref>、[[山城国]][[一宮]]の[[賀茂別雷神社]]の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の[[吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|吉祥院]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。
[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の神社が合祀された。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == * [[宮原駅]]より徒歩8分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
== 関連項目 == * [[上尾宿]] * [[中山道]]
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしや}} [[Category:さいたま市の神社]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の歴史]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q106301088}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|加茂神社 (さいたま市)|version=92210585|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 加茂神社 |画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]4-8-1 |位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = |祭神 = [[賀茂別雷命|別雷神]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は[[宮原村 (埼玉県)|宮原村]]および[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]を参照。</ref>、[[山城国]][[一宮]]の[[賀茂別雷神社]]の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の[[吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|吉祥院]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。
[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の神社が合祀された。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == * [[宮原駅]]より徒歩8分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
== 関連項目 == * [[上尾宿]] * [[中山道]]

==End Japanese==

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 加茂神社 |画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]][[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]4-8-1 |位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = |祭神 = [[賀茂別雷命|別雷神]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[北区 (さいたま市)|北区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は[[宮原村 (埼玉県)|宮原村]]および[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]を参照。</ref>、[[山城国]][[一宮]]の[[賀茂別雷神社]]の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の[[吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|吉祥院]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。
[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の神社が合祀された。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == * [[宮原駅]]より徒歩8分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
== 関連項目 == * [[上尾宿]] * [[中山道]]
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:さいたま市の神社]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の歴史]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社]]
==End Japanese==

== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は[[宮原村 (埼玉県)|宮原村]]および[[宮原町 (さいたま市)|宮原町]]を参照。</ref>、[[山城国]][[一宮]]の[[賀茂別雷神社]]の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の[[吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|吉祥院]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。
[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の神社が合祀された。[[1919年]]（[[大正]]8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == * [[宮原駅]]より徒歩8分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
== 関連項目 == * [[上尾宿]] * [[中山道]]
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:さいたま市の神社]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の歴史]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社]]

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 加茂神社 |画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}4-8-1 |位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = |祭神 = {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|別雷神}} |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は{{jalink|宮原村 (埼玉県)|Q11453404|宮原村}}および{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}を参照。</ref>、{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}の{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の{{jalink|吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|Q124483193|吉祥院}}が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。
{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により、周辺の神社が合祀された。{{jalink|1919年|Q2157}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == * {{jalink|宮原駅|Q845070}}より徒歩8分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
== 関連項目 == * {{jalink|上尾宿|Q2826787}} * {{jalink|中山道|Q1154479}}
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:さいたま市の神社]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の歴史]] [[Category:さいたま市北区の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社]]

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 加茂神社
|画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}4-8-1
|位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|地図   = 
|祭神   = {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|別雷神}}
|社格   = 村社
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は{{jalink|宮原村 (埼玉県)|Q11453404|宮原村}}および{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}を参照。</ref>、{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}の{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の{{jalink|吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|Q124483193|吉祥院}}が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により、周辺の神社が合祀された。{{jalink|1919年|Q2157}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
* {{jalink|宮原駅|Q845070}}より徒歩8分。

[[JA:加茂神社 (さいたま市)]]

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 加茂神社
|画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}4-8-1
|位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|地図   = 
|祭神   = {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|別雷神}}
|社格   = 村社
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は{{jalink|宮原村 (埼玉県)|Q11453404|宮原村}}および{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}を参照。</ref>、{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}の{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の{{jalink|吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|Q124483193|吉祥院}}が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により、周辺の神社が合祀された。{{jalink|1919年|Q2157}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
* {{jalink|宮原駅|Q845070}}より徒歩8分。

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 加茂神社
|画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}4-8-1
|位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|地図   = 
|祭神   = {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|別雷神}}
|社格   = 村社
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は{{jalink|宮原村 (埼玉県)|Q11453404|宮原村}}および{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}を参照。</ref>、{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}の{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の{{jalink|吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|Q124483193|吉祥院}}が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により、周辺の神社が合祀された。{{jalink|1919年|Q2157}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
* {{jalink|宮原駅|Q845070}}より徒歩8分。

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 加茂神社
|画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}4-8-1
|位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|地図   = 
|祭神   = {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|別雷神}}
|社格   = 村社
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は{{jalink|宮原村 (埼玉県)|Q11453404|宮原村}}および{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}を参照。</ref>、{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}の{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の{{jalink|吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|Q124483193|吉祥院}}が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により、周辺の神社が合祀された。{{jalink|1919年|Q2157}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
* {{jalink|宮原駅|Q845070}}より徒歩8分。

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 加茂神社
|画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}4-8-1
|位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|地図   = 
|祭神   = {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|別雷神}}
|社格   = 村社
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は{{jalink|宮原村 (埼玉県)|Q11453404|宮原村}}および{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}を参照。</ref>、{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}の{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の{{jalink|吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|Q124483193|吉祥院}}が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により、周辺の神社が合祀された。{{jalink|1919年|Q2157}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
* {{jalink|宮原駅|Q845070}}より徒歩8分。

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 加茂神社
|画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}4-8-1
|位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|地図   = 
|祭神   = {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|別雷神}}
|社格   = 村社
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は{{jalink|宮原村 (埼玉県)|Q11453404|宮原村}}および{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}を参照。</ref>、{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}の{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の{{jalink|吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|Q124483193|吉祥院}}が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により、周辺の神社が合祀された。{{jalink|1919年|Q2157}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
* {{jalink|宮原駅|Q845070}}より徒歩8分。

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 加茂神社
|画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}4-8-1
|位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|地図   = 
|祭神   = {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|別雷神}}
|社格   = 村社
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は{{jalink|宮原村 (埼玉県)|Q11453404|宮原村}}および{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}を参照。</ref>、{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}の{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の{{jalink|吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|Q124483193|吉祥院}}が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により、周辺の神社が合祀された。{{jalink|1919年|Q2157}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
* {{jalink|宮原駅|Q845070}}より徒歩8分。

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 加茂神社
|画像=[[File:Kamo Jinja (Kita, Saitama).JPG|250px]]
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}4-8-1
|位置 = {{Coord|35|56|43.74|N|139|36|32.17|E|region:JP-11_scale:10000_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|地図   = 
|祭神   = {{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|別雷神}}
|社格   = 村社
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''加茂神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|さいたま市|Q134635}}{{jalink|北区 (さいたま市)|Q542542|北区}}の{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 歴史 ===
創建年代は不明である。ただ当地の地名「{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}」の「宮」の由来であり<ref>詳細は{{jalink|宮原村 (埼玉県)|Q11453404|宮原村}}および{{jalink|宮原町 (さいたま市)|Q11453414|宮原町}}を参照。</ref>、{{jalink|山城国|Q749276}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}の{{jalink|賀茂別雷神社|Q700448}}の分霊を勧請したものという。同市同区の{{jalink|吉祥院 (さいたま市北区)|Q124483193|吉祥院}}が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、224-225p</ref>。

{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}に基づく「村社」に列せられ、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により、周辺の神社が合祀された。{{jalink|1919年|Q2157}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}8年）に拝殿が焼失している<ref name="神社"></ref>。

=== 交通アクセス ===
* {{jalink|宮原駅|Q845070}}より徒歩8分。

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|WD=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|WD=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|WD=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|WD=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|WD=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|WD=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|WD=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|賀茂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

'''Kamo Shrine''' (??????) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Saitama)|Q542542|qid=Q542542|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama|Q134635|qid=Q134635|1=Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
Its founding date is unknown. However, it is said to be the origin of the "miya" in the local place name {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|qid=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}<ref>For details, see {{ill|lt=Miyahara Village (Saitama Prefecture)|Q11453404|qid=Q11453404|lt=Miyahara Village|1=Miyahara}} and {{ill|lt=Miyahara-ch? (Saitama)|Q11453414|qid=Q11453414|lt=Miyahara-ch?|1=Miyaharachō}}.</ref>, and to have been founded by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|lt=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|qid=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|qid=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} Ichinomiya. {{ill|lt=Kissh?in (Kita-ku, Saitama)|Q124483193|qid=Q124483193|lt=Kissh?in|1=Kissh?in}} in the same ward served as its {{ill|lt=bett?ji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}<ref name="??">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office Shrine Survey Group (ed.), ''Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama'', Saitama Prefecture Shrine Office, 1998, pp. 224-225</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and during shrine mergers in the late Meiji period, surrounding shrines were merged into it. In {{ill|lt=1919|Q2157|qid=Q2157|1=1919}} (Taish? 8), the worship hall burned down<ref name="??"></ref>.

=== Access ===
* 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Miyahara Station|Q845070|qid=Q845070|1=Miyahara Station}}.
